pages.title: About Jeremy | Jeremy Wright
pages.meta_description: My parents were both teachers – dad taught maths and physics and mum taught music. My brother, who is two years younger, and I went to the local primary school and then I won a scholarship to Taunton School. There I sat A levels in English, History and Latin and went on to read Law at Exeter University. My brother rose to become a Lieutenant Commander in the Royal Navy and is now a teacher.
headings: h2: Main navigation
h2: About Jeremy
h3: Sir Jeremy Wright MP Kenilworth and Southam
h2: Footer
pages.text_excerpt: About Jeremy | Jeremy Wright Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Jeremy News In Parliament HS2 Campaign Responses Contact About Jeremy Tweet My parents were both teachers. My brother and I went to the local primary school, and then I won a scholarship to Taunton School. From there, I went on to read Law at Exeter University. As a family, we were not party political, but we always talked about current affairs, so I became interested in politics. After university I pursued a career in the law because I had always wanted to be an advocate. I have always derived great satisfaction and a sense of fulfilment from helping someone to make their case, but I began to think how much better it would be if, rather than just representing one person as a barrister, I could represent thousands of people as their Member of Parliament. That led to me seeking selection as a prospective parliamentary candidate for the Conservatives. I was delighted to be chosen to represent the party in Rugby and Kenilworth in 2003 and even more delighted to win the seat back from Labour in 2005. Following a Boundary Review, I have been elected as MP for Kenilworth and Southam in 2010, 2015, 2017, 2019 and 2024. In Opposition, I was a member of the Justice Select Committee, and I founded the All-Party Parliamentary Group (APPG) on Dementia. I chaired the group until 2010, when my appointment to the Government, as a Whip, meant that I could no longer continue in this role. In 2012 I was appointed to the position of Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State at the Ministry of Justice with responsibility for the prison service, probation, rehabilitation and sentencing. In the Government reshuffle in July 2014, I was promoted to be Attorney General, chief legal adviser to the Government. The role also oversees the Crown Prosecution Service, the Serious Fraud Office and Her Majesty’s Crown Prosecution Service Inspectorate. I then served as Secretary of State at the Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport from 9th July 2018 until the new Prime Minister took office on 24th July 2019. From November 2019 to November 2022, I was a member of the Committee on Standards in Public Life. I was knighted in the Queen’s 2022 Birthday Honours for political and public service. I chaired the APPG on Digital Regulation and Responsibility from November 2019 - July 2024 and was a member of The Intelligence and Security Committee of Parliament (ISC) from February 2022 to May 2024. I returned to serving on the Committee in December 2024 and am now its Deputy Chairman. I served as Shadow Attorney General from July 2024 to November 2024 and since November 2024 I serve as Deputy Chairman of the Speaker’s Conference on the safety of Candidates and MPs, as a member of the Speaker’s Committee on the Electoral Commission and of the Steering Group on AI in Parliaments. I have also been appointed to the House of Commons Panel of Chairs. Polling Stations: Religious Buildings | Speaker's Committee on the Electoral Commission | Written Answers Individual local authorities are responsible for designating polling places. Polling Stations: Religious Buildings | Speaker's Committee on the Electoral Commission | Written Answers The Commission provides guidance to Returning Officers on identifying suitable polling station venues. Elections | Speaker's Committee on the Electoral Commission | Written Answers The Electoral Commission does not have a memorandum of understanding with the Metropolitan Police. pages.text_excerpt: Campaign Responses | Jeremy Wright Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Jeremy News In Parliament HS2 Campaign Responses Contact Campaign Responses Tweet Coming soon... Social Media Ban for Under 16s - April 26 Thank you for contacting me about the proposed social media ban for under-16s. Stop the Holiday Tax campaign - April 26 Thank you for contacting me about the new levy on overnight trips. Crime and Policing Bill (Amendment 312) - April 26 Thank you for contacting me about legislative changes on cumulative disruption in the Crime and Policing Bill. Railways Bill - April 26 Thank you for contacting me about the Railways Bill. Women Against State Pension Inequality (WASPI) campaign - April 26 Thank you for contacting me about the campaign for Women Against State Pension Inequality (WASPI). After much delay the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions gave a statement on 29th January 2026 reaffirming their decision to not compensate those impacted. Sewage and Storm Overflows - April 26 Thank you for contacting me about sewage and storm overflows. The Channel 4 programme, 'Dirty Business’ raised awareness to some of the concerns about sewage spills. Plan 2 Student Loans - April 26 Thank you for contacting me about plan 2 student loans. Freezing the threshold for repayment will mean that as graduates' starting salaries increase with inflation, they will end up paying more and earlier. Factory Farming campaign - April 26 Thank you for contacting me about factory farming. Social Housing - April 26 Thank you for contacting me about social housing.It is vital the Government works to build more homes, including social and affordable homes. This is why I am broadly supportive of the Government’s overarching ambition to build 1.5 million new homes. Online Gambling - April 26 Thank you for contacting me about online gambling. Requirements for meat and dairy servings at schools - April 26 Thank you for contacting me about school food. King's Guards ceremonial caps - April 26 Thank you for contacting me about the King's Guards ceremonial caps. I do not generally sign EDMs but I am aware of the People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals’ (PETA) ongoing campaign to replace the real fur used in the King’s Guards' ceremonial bearskin caps with faux fur. pages.text_excerpt: Contact | Sir Jeremy Wright MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Jeremy News In Parliament HS2 Campaign Responses Contact Contact Leave this field blank Constituency Office Jubilee House, Smalley Place, Kenilworth, CV8 1QG 01926 853650 [email protected] Westminster Office House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA 020 7219 2008 [email protected] Jeremy Wright MP Jeremy Wright MP The quickest way to get in touch with me is to send a message by completing the form below. It is also helpful if you provide a contact telephone number. First Name Last Name Postcode Address id Address 1 Email Mobile number Message I would like to be kept up-to-date by Jeremy Wright on future issues that matter to me: Yes There will be an easy way to opt out of future messages. For more information on how we handle your data and your rights, please see our Privacy Policy . Volunteer for us In which of these ways can you help us? Delivering a few leaflets locally now and again Helping with office and admin tasks Making calls to voters and volunteers Joining our canvassing team to knock on doors Potentially standing to be a local Conservative Councillor Becoming a Digital Ambassador By agreeing to volunteer for us, you also agree to us contacting you about volunteering. For more information, please see our Data Protection and Privacy Policy Due to a strict parliamentary protocol I am only able to help those who live in the Kenilworth and Southam constituency, I am not able to intervene in matters raised by the constituents of another constituency. The information you provide is used in accordance with our Data Protection and Privacy Policy . By clicking this button you agree to your information being used in accordance with that policy. pages.text_excerpt: Surgeries | Jeremy Wright Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Jeremy News In Parliament HS2 Campaign Responses Contact Surgeries Tweet I have regular surgery appointments available for constituents. I want to stress that, whatever the problem is, my team and I will try to help in any way that we can. Where we cannot or unable, we will be transparent and honest. If you would like to a request an appointment, please email me at [email protected] or contact my office on 01926 853650. Strict Parliamentary protocol states that I can only deal with constituents of Kenilworth and Southam, so please include your full name, address (including postcode) and contact details in the email. Please also include details of the issue you would like to discuss. This will all be kept fully confidential.
